subject: Fwd: Galisteo Basin Archaeological Sites Protection Act
body:

Darren K.  Indyke

************************************** See what's new at http://www.aol.com


From: Zorroranch@aol.com
Subject: Fwd: Galisteo Basin Archaeological Sites Protection Act
Date: Fri, 16 Nov 2007 23:24:23 GMT
To: DKIESQ@aol.com

FYI



************************************** See what's new at http://www.aol.com


From: Paul_Williams@nm.blm.gov
Subject: Re: Galisteo Basin Archaeological Sites Protection Act
Date: Fri, 16 Nov 2007 22:40:34 GMT
To: Zorroranch@aol.com

Karen and Bryce,

   I just wanted to send a note to update you on a Galisteo Basin
   Archaeological Sites Protection Act (GBASPA) project.   This year the NM
   State Legislature passed New Mexico House Bill 74.  The purpose of the
   Bill is to prepare site assessments of the twenty-four archaeological
   sites named in the GBASPA.  The Office of Archaeological Studies, a
   division of the New Mexico Department of Cultural Affairs, is in the
   process of issuing contracts with archaeologists to perform these
   assessments.  A small company, Aspen CRM Solutions, will be doing the
   assessment of Comanche Gap.  The Office of Contract Archaeology at UNM
   will be doing the Assessment of Pueblo Blanco.  They will not need
   access across Zorro Ranch to get to Pueblo Blanco, but we wanted you to
   know about the project and give permission to carry out a little field
   work on Zorro Ranch property.  I have given Aspen CRM Solutions your
   phone number so that they can contact you to arrange a visit to Comanche
   Gap.  I’m not sure whether they will need more than one field day to
   complete their work.

   The site assessments will be an important step in the plan that is
   called for in the GBASPA.  Each site will get an updated site form, and
   the location of each site will be plotted on aerial photographs.  Site
   protection and conservation needs will also be addressed, which will
   help us prepare budget requests for future federal funding.

   I am sending a letter with more information.  If you have any questions
   please call me at 751-4710.

   Paul

From:Zorroranch@aol.com
Subject:Fwd: Galisteo Basin Archaeological Sites Protection Act
Date:Fri, 16 Nov 2007 23:24:23 GMT
To:DKIESQ@aol.com

FYI



See what's new at AOL.com and Make AOL Your Homepage.

From:Paul_Williams@nm.blm.gov
Subject:Re: Galisteo Basin Archaeological Sites Protection Act
Date:Fri, 16 Nov 2007 22:40:34 GMT
To:Zorroranch@aol.com

Karen and Bryce,

I just wanted to send a note to update you on a Galisteo Basin
Archaeological Sites Protection Act (GBASPA) project. This year the NM
State Legislature passed New Mexico House Bill 74. The purpose of the
Bill is to prepare site assessments of the twenty-four archaeological
sites named in the GBASPA. The Office of Archaeological Studies, a
division of the New Mexico Department of Cultural Affairs, is in the
process of issuing contracts with archaeologists to perform these
assessments. A small company, Aspen CRM Solutions, will be doing the
assessment of Comanche Gap. The Office of Contract Archaeology at UNM
will be doing the Assessment of Pueblo Blanco. They will not need
access across Zorro Ranch to get to Pueblo Blanco, but we wanted you to
know about the project and give permission to carry out a little field
work on Zorro Ranch property. I have given Aspen CRM Solutions your
phone number so that they can contact you to arrange a visit to Comanche
Gap. I’m not sure whether they will need more than one field day to
complete their work.

The site assessments will be an important step in the plan that is
called for in the GBASPA. Each site will get an updated site form, and
the location of each site will be plotted on aerial photographs. Site
protection and conservation needs will also be addressed, which will
help us prepare budget requests for future federal funding.

I am sending a letter with more information. If you have any questions
please call me at 751-4710.

Paul
